note: Remove HTTP Headers exposes a single config object, one permission, an HTTP stack middleware, and a small ConfigManager API. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) since they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want to control which HTTP response headers the Remove HTTP Headers module strips. Which config object and key hold the list?
evals.expected_output: The headers_to_remove key (a sequence of header-name strings) on the remove_http_headers.settings config object.

evals.prompt: Which HTTP response headers does the Remove HTTP Headers module remove by default on a fresh install?
evals.expected_output: By default headers_to_remove contains X-Generator, X-Drupal-Dynamic-Cache, and X-Drupal-Cache.

evals.prompt: I want to hide the Drupal version fingerprint from my site's responses. Which header should I remove with the Remove HTTP Headers module, and what extra effect does removing it have on the HTML?
evals.expected_output: Remove the X-Generator header; when it is in the list the module also strips Drupal's default <meta name="Generator"> tag from the HTML <head> via hook_page_attachments_alter().

evals.prompt: How does the Remove HTTP Headers module actually strip the configured headers from responses on a Drupal 11 site — what mechanism runs?
evals.expected_output: An HTTP stack middleware (RemoveHttpHeadersMiddleware, tagged http_middleware with priority 1000 so it runs last) calls $response->headers->remove() for each configured header on the main request.

evals.prompt: What is the admin path and route name of the Remove HTTP Headers settings form on a Drupal site?
Which permission gates access to the Remove HTTP Headers settings form?
evals.expected_output: The 'remove_http_headers settings access' permission (restrict access: TRUE) gates the settings form.

evals.prompt: I want to add a custom header name to the Remove HTTP Headers removal list without using the UI. What are two ways to do it via configuration?
evals.expected_output: Edit the headers_to_remove sequence in remove_http_headers.settings.yml and drush config:import, or set it with drush config:set remove_http_headers.settings; the UI textarea is one header per line.
